entries.speaker: gate_guard
lines:
Commander Edren. You've been expected. The king
received word from Thornwatch.

entries.speaker: edren
lines: Commander Halda sent ahead?

entries.speaker: gate_guard
lines:
A rider, two days ago. The king's been asking
for you since.

entries.speaker: gate_guard
lines:
Your... company will need to register at the
inner gate.

entries.speaker: sable
lines: Of course. I love registering.

entries.speaker: king_aldren
lines:
Edren. You look like you've walked through a
war.

entries.speaker: edren
lines:
Nearly, Your Majesty. We found something in an
Ironmouth mine that the Compact was excavating. A pre-civilization
artifact.

entries.speaker: king_aldren
lines:
This is what Halda's rider described? It
doesn't look like much.

entries.speaker: maren
lines:
It isn't much. That's the danger, Your Majesty. It's
a conduit — a focal point for something beyond it. The miners who
found it are dead. Not from violence. From despair.

entries.speaker: haren
lines: Maren. You've been away from court for some time.

entries.speaker: maren
lines:
Not by choice, Chancellor. I left because my warnings
were ignored. I'm here because this time, ignoring them will cost
more than my career.

entries.speaker: king_aldren
lines:
Every morning I ask the court mages for a
report on the ley lines. Every morning, the number is lower.
I've stopped asking what it means.

entries.speaker: king_aldren
lines:
You brought this to me for safekeeping. I
wonder if you understand — there is nothing safe left in Valdris.

entries.speaker: edren
lines: Safer here than in Compact hands.

entries.speaker: king_aldren
lines:
Perhaps. Maren — I want your assessment in
writing by morning. Cael, you studied ley resonance at the
academy. I'm assigning you to work with Maren on this.

entries.speaker: cael
lines: Of course, Your Majesty.

entries.speaker: king_aldren
lines:
The rest of you — welcome to Valdris. Such as
it is. Find quarters in the Keep and rest. I expect we'll need
you soon.

entries.speaker: aldis
lines:
Commander Edren. I've been cataloguing the ley line
decline for three years now. Twelve percent capacity loss in the
last year alone. I presented these findings to the court. They
asked me if I was certain about the margin of error.

entries.speaker: aldis
lines:
If your artifact is related to the ley line failures,
I'd like to compare my readings. The resonance patterns may
correlate. I'll work with Cael — he has a mind for this kind of
analysis.

entries.speaker: aldis
lines:
You're the Forgewright defector. I read your conduit
efficiency paper — the one the Consortium suppressed. Remarkably
precise work. If you have time, I'd like to discuss your
extraction rate projections.

entries.speaker: lira
lines: You read that? It was classified.

entries.speaker: aldis
lines:
I'm a researcher. Finding classified documents is
half the job.

entries.speaker: aldis
lines: Maren? You're... back?

entries.speaker: maren
lines: Temporarily. Don't rearrange my study while I'm here.

entries.speaker: aldis
lines:
I reorganized it by subject three years ago. It's
much more efficient now.

entries.speaker: maren
lines: I'm sure it is.

entries.speaker: cordwyn
lines: Edren. Good to see you in one piece.

entries.speaker: cordwyn
lines:
Heard you pulled something out of an old mine that
has the whole court whispering. The Chancellor's already trying
to figure out how to use it as a bargaining chip with the
Compact.

entries.speaker: edren
lines: It's not a weapon.

entries.speaker: cordwyn
lines:
Everything's a weapon to a politician. Just make
sure Cael doesn't lose himself in that research. He's brilliant,
but he carries more than he lets on.

entries.speaker: cordwyn
lines:
Keep an eye on him. That's not an order. It's a
request.

entries.speaker: cordwyn
lines: Spirit-speaker. You're a long way from the Wilds.

entries.speaker: torren
lines: The Wilds follow where they're needed. So do I.

entries.speaker: cordwyn
lines:
Good. I like people who don't need to be asked
twice.

entries.speaker: cordwyn
lines: Touch nothing, thief.

entries.speaker: sable
lines: I was admiring. There's a difference.

entries.speaker: cordwyn
lines: There isn't.

entries.speaker: renn
lines:
Sable. You're supposed to be dead in a ditch
somewhere.

entries.speaker: sable
lines: I get that a lot.

entries.speaker: renn
lines:
Sit down. You look like you've been running from
something expensive.

entries.speaker: sable
lines:
Running toward something, actually. I need
information.

entries.speaker: renn
lines:
I don't sell secrets, love. I sell certainty. You
want to know something for sure? That costs extra.

entries.speaker: sable
lines:
What do you know about the Compact's mining
operations in the southern Wilds?

entries.speaker: renn
lines:
I know they found something they weren't expecting.
I know three courier birds went to Corrund in one day. And I
know the price of Forgewright steel just dropped, which means
the generals are spending money on something other than weapons.

entries.speaker: renn
lines:
Whatever you've gotten yourself into, Sable — it's
bigger than a mine.

entries.speaker: sable
lines: Yeah. I'm starting to figure that out.

*Act I complete. The party is assembled, the artifact is in the
worst possible hands, and the stranger on the cliff is already
forgotten.*
*Continue: [Act II Part 1 — The Fraying](act-ii-part-1.md)*
